name: Albuquerque
submarket: Albuquerque / Rio Rancho / Los Lunas / East Mountains
profile: Military-friendly Kirtland AFB housing snapshot anchored to Albuquerque, Rio Rancho, Los Lunas, Tijeras, Edgewood, and the East Mountains, built for PCSUnited base-level market overviews.

market_metrics.market_type_summary: Stable Albuquerque military, national-lab, and defense market with fast-moving well-priced homes and strong neighborhood-by-neighborhood variation

mortgage_assumptions.defaults_note: Based on March 2026 New Mexico VA loan planning assumptions.

notes: Kirtland buyers should model mortgage, taxes, insurance, HOA, utilities, commute, property condition, roof/HVAC age, and neighborhood risk together.
Albuquerque pricing is very neighborhood-specific. Northeast Heights, Four Hills, Rio Rancho, Los Lunas, and East Mountains each produce different commute and lifestyle outcomes.
Desert utilities, cooling, roof condition, drainage, security, and school-zone fit should be checked before treating a payment as safe.

avg_home_mortgage_monthly.notes: VA loan planning estimate for practical Kirtland / Albuquerque-area housing, including New Mexico tax and insurance assumptions.

crime_status.installation: Extremely safe; gated military installation with controlled access.
crime_status.metro_albuquerque: Albuquerque safety varies significantly by neighborhood and block. Military families should evaluate exact address, school zone, commute route, property condition, security, and resale logic before committing.

school_quality.on_base: No full on-base K-12 system should be assumed; families should verify current school options and attendance boundaries by exact housing address.
school_quality.off_base: Albuquerque Public Schools, Rio Rancho Public Schools, Los Lunas Schools, and East Mountain-area options vary by exact address. Families should verify district, school assignment, commute, and support services before choosing housing.

rental_vacancy.notes: Rental inventory is influenced by Kirtland AFB, Sandia National Laboratories, defense contractors, University of New Mexico, healthcare, and broader Albuquerque demand.

rental_metrics.notes: Planning estimate for 3-bedroom military-family rentals across Albuquerque, Rio Rancho, Los Lunas, and nearby Kirtland commute corridors.

military_lifestyle_fit.description: Large defense, nuclear enterprise, research, space, national lab, and contractor ecosystem with strong Albuquerque amenities and broad housing choice across city, suburban, and foothill corridors.

financial_brief.affordability_summary: Kirtland is workable for many VA buyers, but Albuquerque’s neighborhood variation, safety differences, school-zone variation, and property condition require careful screening.
financial_brief.buyer_opportunity: Strongest for disciplined VA buyers comparing Northeast Heights, Four Hills, Rio Rancho, Los Lunas, East Mountains, and selected Albuquerque pockets by commute, safety, schools, and all-in payment.

market_bluf.bluf_summary: Kirtland gives military families a strong Albuquerque housing lane tied to defense, labs, and space missions, but the best move depends on neighborhood selection, commute route, school fit, safety, and property condition.

summary_points: Kirtland AFB sits inside Albuquerque, a major defense, research, nuclear enterprise, space, and national lab market.
Albuquerque has strong housing variety, but neighborhood quality, safety, school zones, and property condition vary sharply.
Homes that are well-priced and move-in ready can move quickly, so users should be prepared without abandoning inspection discipline.
Rio Rancho and Los Lunas can offer suburban value but create different commute and lifestyle tradeoffs.
The best Kirtland housing decision balances BAH, commute, safety, school zones, property condition, utilities, and assignment length.
opportunities: Strong defense, national lab, space, and contractor ecosystem supports long-term housing relevance.
Albuquerque offers broad inventory across urban, suburban, foothill, and rural-edge lifestyles.
Rio Rancho and Los Lunas may provide newer homes or more space for some budgets.
Kirtland’s location allows short commutes from selected southeast, northeast, and east-side pockets.
VA buyers may find solid long-term value when the property, neighborhood, and payment are disciplined.
risks: Neighborhood safety varies significantly by exact address.
School quality and family fit vary by district and attendance boundary.
Older homes may need roof, HVAC, plumbing, electrical, windows, or drainage review.
Rio Rancho, Los Lunas, or East Mountains commutes can become longer than expected.
Short assignments may favor renting if resale timing, property condition, or neighborhood confidence is weak.
buyer_notes: Kirtland buyers should compare Albuquerque pockets, Northeast Heights, Four Hills, Rio Rancho, Los Lunas, Tijeras, Edgewood, and East Mountains by commute, safety, school zones, condition, and all-in payment.
Do not choose by purchase price alone; address-level safety, condition, and resale matter heavily in Albuquerque.
A clean, well-maintained home in a stronger pocket may be better than a cheaper property with weak condition or neighborhood risk.
Rio Rancho and Los Lunas can offer more suburban value, but commute must be tested during real report-time windows.
This market rewards buyers who preserve residual income and avoid emotional bidding on homes that do not pass the full PCS risk screen.
seller_notes: Sellers should position homes around commute, VA readiness, safety/neighborhood confidence, school-zone clarity, roof/HVAC condition, and practical monthly value.
Move-in-ready homes with updated roof, HVAC, windows, drainage, and clean inspection confidence can stand out.
Military buyers respond well to clear maintenance history, PCS-friendly timing, and realistic payment logic.
Homes in strong commute corridors should emphasize access to Kirtland, Sandia Labs, the airport, and defense-contractor employment when relevant.
investor_angles: Military, national lab, defense-contractor, university, healthcare, and workforce demand support rental relevance across Albuquerque.
Cash flow depends heavily on neighborhood selection, insurance, maintenance, taxes, and tenant profile.
Investors should stress-test vacancy, repairs, security, roof/HVAC replacement, tenant turnover, and property management.
Clean single-family homes in practical commute corridors may outperform cheaper properties in weaker pockets.
buyer_guidance: Use all-in monthly cost as the primary decision filter.
Compare Albuquerque, Rio Rancho, Los Lunas, Tijeras, Edgewood, and East Mountains by commute, school zone, safety, utilities, and property condition.
Verify roof age, HVAC condition, insulation, windows, drainage, cooling efficiency, utility expectations, and neighborhood risk before finalizing a purchase budget.
Avoid choosing solely by lowest price if the home has weak condition, safety concerns, or limited resale logic.
Use PCSUnited calculators to compare rent, buy, residual income, commute risk, desert utility pressure, and assignment-length exposure.
seller_guidance: Price against current buyer payment sensitivity and active Albuquerque inventory.
Highlight commute logic, VA readiness, roof condition, HVAC quality, cooling efficiency, security, and low-maintenance desert features.
Homes with updated roof, HVAC, windows, insulation, and clean inspection history should be positioned clearly.
Military buyers need practical value and confidence in monthly cost, not generic listing language.
landlord_notes: Military, lab, contractor, university, and healthcare renter demand can support stable occupancy near Kirtland and Albuquerque job centers.
Single-family rentals in good condition and practical commute corridors may appeal to military families avoiding purchase risk.
Investors should model HVAC maintenance, vacancy, turnover, utility efficiency, security, and repairs conservatively.
Clean, functional homes near base, services, and schools may outperform cheaper but weaker-condition inventory.

market.market_type_summary: Stable Albuquerque defense-tech market with fast-moving well-priced homes and high neighborhood-level variation

base_profile.installation_type: Research / Test / Nuclear Enterprise / Space / Mission Support Base
base_profile.host_or_major_command: 377th Air Base Wing / Air Force Global Strike Command
base_profile.primary_mission_summary: Kirtland AFB is a major Air Force installation in Albuquerque supporting nuclear enterprise missions, research and development, space and rapid capabilities, operational test and evaluation, defense threat reduction, mission support, and close integration with Sandia National Laboratories and the Albuquerque defense ecosystem.
base_profile.pcs_personality: Defense-tech, national-lab-driven, desert metro, commute-sensitive, neighborhood-specific, school-zone-sensitive, and strongly tied to Albuquerque, Rio Rancho, Los Lunas, Four Hills, Northeast Heights, and East Mountains housing choices.
base_profile.base_bluf: Kirtland is not just an Albuquerque housing decision. It is a neighborhood-selection, commute-route, school-zone, safety, roof/HVAC, desert-utility, BAH, and assignment-length decision. The market can work very well, but address-level screening matters more here than broad metro averages.

user_positioning.military_family_bluf: Prioritize exact-address safety, school-zone verification, commute route, roof/HVAC condition, BAH fit, and property condition before signing a lease or purchase contract.
user_positioning.single_airman_bluf: Prioritize commute simplicity, rent control, roommate flexibility, daily services, parking, and avoiding higher-risk pockets without strong reason.
user_positioning.realtor_bluf: The strongest Kirtland housing conversations are about exact neighborhood fit, commute, safety, VA-ready condition, school-zone confidence, and defense/lab employment stability.
user_positioning.investor_bluf: Rental demand near Kirtland is influenced by military, Sandia Labs, defense contractors, UNM, healthcare, and workforce demand, but returns depend heavily on neighborhood selection and maintenance control.

gates.name: Eubank Gate
gates.hours: Verify official Kirtland AFB gate hours before travel
gates.commute_notes: Useful for users living in Northeast Heights, Four Hills, east Albuquerque, and foothill-adjacent corridors.
gates.user_warning: Gate hours and route suitability should be verified before relying on this gate.

gates.hours: Verify official Kirtland AFB gate hours before travel
gates.commute_notes: May support certain commute patterns depending on home location, duty location, and current access rules.

gates.hours: Verify official Kirtland AFB gate hours before travel

major_services.user_note: Military families should contact the housing office early for current local housing conditions, referral services, and relocation support.

major_services.address: 1012 Golden Smoke Drive, Kirtland AFB, NM 87116
major_services.email: Verify current leasing contact through housing provider
major_services.user_note: On-base housing can simplify commute and reduce neighborhood-selection uncertainty, but families should compare BAH capture, waitlists, pet policies, and off-base alternatives.

major_services.address: Verify current office location before travel
major_services.email: Verify current email with official installation sources
major_services.user_note: Eligibility and availability should be verified directly with the housing office.

major_services.address: Verify current branch or ATM location before travel

major_services.address: Verify current office location before travel

major_services.address: Verify current M&FRC location before travel
major_services.user_note: Critical for families and newcomers. Exact program availability, waitlists, and eligibility should be verified with official Kirtland resources.

on_base_housing.housing_type: Privatized and military housing support environment
on_base_housing.housing_office_hours: Monday-Friday, 9:00 a.m. to 3:00 p.m.; closed weekends, holidays, and wing training days; verify current hours
on_base_housing.privatized_housing_provider: Kirtland Family Housing / Hunt Military Communities
on_base_housing.map_zone: housing support area / Kirtland Family Housing
best_for: Families wanting to reduce commute uncertainty
Users wanting to reduce neighborhood-selection uncertainty
watchouts: Availability and waitlists can change
On-base housing may capture BAH differently than off-base renting
Pet policies and breed restrictions should be verified
School assignment must be verified by exact address
Off-base options in Albuquerque, Rio Rancho, Los Lunas, and East Mountains should be compared carefully
Neighborhood safety, commute route, utilities, and property condition should be modeled for off-base purchase scenarios
on_base_housing.pcsu_strategy_note: Compare on-base housing against off-base rent and mortgage options. Around Kirtland, the best choice depends on rank, dependents, BAH, debt, commute tolerance, school priorities, neighborhood confidence, roof/HVAC condition, pets, and expected assignment length.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Four Hills
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Highly practical east/southeast Albuquerque corridor with strong Kirtland commute logic and family appeal.
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 10-25 minutes depending on exact address, gate, and traffic
likely_gate_logic: Gibson Gate depending on route and duty location
watchouts: Prices can be higher than some Albuquerque pockets
Older homes may require roof/HVAC inspection
Exact school zone and property condition should be verified
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Strong first-look area for families and buyers who value commute, neighborhood feel, and resale logic.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Northeast Heights
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Large and popular Albuquerque corridor with broad services, school options, and stronger family appeal in selected pockets.
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 20-40 minutes depending on exact address, route, gate, and traffic
likely_gate_logic: Wyoming Gate depending on route and duty location
watchouts: Pricing varies significantly by pocket
Commute should be tested during report-time windows
Older homes may need systems review
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Good family corridor when the exact pocket, price, and commute fit the household.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: East Albuquerque Selected Pockets
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Close practical option with short commute potential, but exact-address safety and property condition are critical.
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 10-25 minutes depending on exact address and gate
watchouts: Neighborhood safety varies by block
Older homes may need stronger inspection
School and resale fit should be reviewed carefully
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Useful commute-first candidate only when the specific pocket and property pass the risk screen.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Rio Rancho
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Large suburban alternative with newer inventory and family appeal, but commute to Kirtland is materially longer.
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 35-60+ minutes depending on exact address, route, gate, and traffic
likely_gate_logic: Eubank Gate depending on route and duty location
watchouts: Commute can become a daily quality-of-life issue
Some areas have infrastructure and distance tradeoffs
Route reliability should be tested carefully
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Good for families prioritizing suburban space and schools, but commute must be accepted upfront.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Los Lunas
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: More affordable south-metro option with newer-home potential, but commute and I-25 exposure require testing.
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 35-60+ minutes depending on exact address, route, gate, and I-25 traffic
likely_gate_logic: Truman Gate depending on route and access status
watchouts: I-25 commute can be draining
Distance from Albuquerque services may matter
Newer subdivisions may include HOA and infrastructure tradeoffs
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Good affordability option for households that intentionally accept the commute.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Tijeras / East Mountains
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Scenic mountain-edge alternative with space and lifestyle appeal, but weather, roads, utilities, and commute require extra diligence.
best_for: Experienced buyers comfortable with rural-edge details
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 30-55+ minutes depending on exact address, road conditions, gate, and weather
likely_gate_logic: Gibson Gate depending on route and duty location
watchouts: Winter weather, road access, wildfire risk, well/septic, and internet should be verified
Commute can be affected by mountain road conditions
Resale market may be more specialized
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Best for users intentionally seeking mountain lifestyle and accepting commute, utility, fire, and maintenance tradeoffs.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Nob Hill / University Selected Pockets
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Urban lifestyle option with access to restaurants, UNM, and city amenities, but parking, safety, and property condition require careful screening.
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 15-30 minutes depending on exact address, gate, and traffic
likely_gate_logic: Truman Gate depending on route and access status
watchouts: Parking and security should be evaluated
Older homes may require stronger inspection
School fit may not match every family profile
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Good lifestyle option for users who want urban access and are comfortable screening property and neighborhood risk closely.

commute_intelligence.overall_commute_posture: Manageable from selected Albuquerque and southeast/east-side pockets, moderate from Northeast Heights, and more challenging from Rio Rancho, Los Lunas, or East Mountains.
primary_commute_risks: Longer commutes from Rio Rancho or Los Lunas
Gate selection based on actual duty location
Choosing by price without checking safety, school, and resale
Monsoon drainage and road conditions in certain pockets
planning_guidance: Confirm the user’s duty location and best gate before choosing a neighborhood.
Test the commute during the user’s expected report-time window.
Compare Albuquerque, Rio Rancho, Los Lunas, Tijeras, and Edgewood by route reliability, schools, safety, and property condition.
Include fuel, time, utilities, HVAC, and maintenance exposure in the housing decision.
Use commute plus all-in housing cost as the core PCSUnited decision screen.
commute_intelligence.pcsu_bluf: Kirtland commute decisions are neighborhood-specific. A home can be financially attractive but wrong if the route, safety, school zone, or property condition does not fit.

bah_market_reality.buying_power_summary: BAH can support buying for many Kirtland families, especially when users avoid overpaying in stronger pockets or buying weak-condition homes in higher-risk areas.
bah_market_reality.renting_summary: Renting remains practical for short assignments, users uncertain about Albuquerque neighborhood fit, or households still learning commute and school priorities.
watch_items: Commute from Rio Rancho or Los Lunas
bah_market_reality.pcsu_strategy_note: Kirtland should be modeled as a favorable but address-sensitive market. PCSUnited should test rank, BAH, debt, savings, credit score, family size, duty location, commute tolerance, neighborhood confidence, condition, and assignment length before recommending buy versus rent.

family_readiness.family_fit: High when the neighborhood is chosen carefully
strengths: Large defense and national lab employment ecosystem
Broad housing choice across city, suburb, and mountain-edge lifestyles
Access to healthcare, universities, shopping, culture, and outdoor recreation
Potentially manageable commute from selected east/southeast Albuquerque pockets
Rio Rancho and Los Lunas provide suburban alternatives
watchouts: Safety and school quality vary sharply by exact address
Commute can be frustrating from outer suburbs
Older homes may need roof/HVAC/plumbing/electrical review
Summer heat, monsoon drainage, and desert maintenance matter
Short assignments require careful rent-versus-buy analysis
family_readiness.pcsu_family_bluf: Kirtland can be a strong family PCS location when the household chooses the right neighborhood, verifies schools and safety, protects residual income, and avoids weak-condition properties.

arrival_checklist: Confirm the user's actual duty location and primary gate before comparing neighborhoods.
Verify current gate hours and visitor access procedures through official Kirtland AFB sources.
Contact the Kirtland Military Housing Office early for current local housing conditions and relocation support.
Compare Albuquerque, Four Hills, Northeast Heights, Rio Rancho, Los Lunas, Tijeras, Edgewood, and East Mountains using commute, schools, safety, condition, and all-in monthly cost.
Review roof, HVAC, plumbing, electrical, insulation, drainage, cooling efficiency, security, and utility expectations before buying.
Verify school zones by exact property address.
Use PCSUnited calculators to compare rent, buy, residual income, assignment length, commute risk, and neighborhood risk before committing.
pcs_watchouts: Do not choose by purchase price alone.
Do not ignore exact-address safety and school-zone fit.
Do not assume Rio Rancho or Los Lunas commute will feel easy without testing it.
Verify roof, HVAC, drainage, utilities, and property condition before committing.
Short assignments may favor renting or on-base housing if resale risk is high.
A cheaper home in the wrong pocket can become more expensive than a higher-priced clean home in a stronger corridor.

realtor_intelligence.best_client_positioning: Lead with neighborhood screening, commute strategy, VA-ready condition, school-zone confidence, safety context, and all-in monthly cost. Kirtland clients need Albuquerque-specific risk filtering, not generic metro sales talk.
strong_listing_angles: Access to Sandia Labs, airport, and defense contractors
Updated roof, HVAC, windows, drainage, and cooling efficiency
buyer_consultation_angles: Rent versus buy based on assignment length
All-in payment including taxes, insurance, HOA, utilities, and maintenance
Albuquerque versus Rio Rancho versus Los Lunas tradeoffs
Future resale appeal to military, lab, and civilian buyers
investor_consultation_angles: Sandia Labs and healthcare workforce demand
realtor_intelligence.pcsu_realtor_bluf: A strong Kirtland realtor should sound like a neighborhood-risk advisor, commute strategist, VA-condition filter, and defense-market financial guide — not just an Albuquerque salesperson.

notes: Market and housing values are PCSUnited planning estimates and should be refreshed periodically.
Gate, visitor, service, medical, and housing office details should be verified against official installation sources before publication or use in routing.
Placeholder map image is used until a dedicated Kirtland AFB base orientation map is created.
Kirtland-area pricing and commute experience vary materially by Albuquerque neighborhood, Rio Rancho, Los Lunas, and East Mountains.

source_notes: Use official Kirtland AFB sources for gate hours, visitor control, newcomer guidance, and installation access.
Use official Air Force Housing, Military OneSource, Kirtland Military Housing Office, and Kirtland Family Housing sources for on-base housing and privatized housing details.
Use DeCA, AAFES, and TRICARE sources for commissary, exchange, and medical details.
Use Zillow-style indexes, Realtor.com-style market data, local MLS, rental aggregators, HUD, and Census-style demographic sources for housing and market refreshes.
base_profile.disclaimer: PCSUnited base profiles are planning tools only. They are not official DoD, Air Force, installation, legal, financial, real estate, lending, insurance, school-zone, safety, or navigation guidance. Users should verify gate access, housing rules, school zones, medical services, visitor procedures, market prices, insurance estimates, commute conditions, utility costs, neighborhood safety, roof/HVAC condition, and property condition through official and professional sources before making decisions.

demographics.provider: US Census Bureau / Albuquerque and Bernalillo County regional planning estimates

rent.provider: Local rental listing estimates / Albuquerque-area rental observations

base_profile.provider: PCSUnited planning profile using public-facing installation, housing, and market research structure

parser_notes: Built in the PCSUnited city/base JSON pattern used by Lackland, Andrews, Barksdale, Beale, Cannon, Charleston, Davis-Monthan, Dover, Dyess, Eglin, Elmendorf, F.E. Warren, Fairchild, Holloman, Hurlburt, and Keesler.
All major PCSUnited fields included and adapted for Kirtland AFB.
Compatible with PCS Snapshot, Base Demographics Shell, Base SaaS, and TOR parsing.
Preserves duplicate value aliases for avg_home_value, average_home_value, avgHome, and city_avg_home.
Preserves duplicate median price aliases for median_list_price, median_sold_price, and median_sale_price.
Preserves by_bedroom entries for 2, 3, 4, and 5-bedroom Market Insight compatibility.

Expanded base_profile to include map image, official links, visitor control center, gates, major services, on-base housing, recommended neighborhoods, commute intelligence, BAH market reality, family readiness, arrival checklist, PCS watchouts, realtor intelligence, data confidence, source notes, and disclaimer.
